article.title: Multi-state physician group enters $4.6M False Claims Act settlement over upcoded E&M visits
article.summary: A 32-provider primary-care group has settled with DOJ and OIG for $4.6M plus a Corporate Integrity Agreement after an audit found systematic upcoding of established-patient E&M visits over a 4-year period. The CIA includes annual coding audits and IRO oversight for 5 years.
article.rawContent: The Department of Justice and HHS-OIG announced a False Claims Act settlement with Sunbelt Primary Care, a 32-provider group operating across three states. The settlement resolves allegations that providers routinely billed level-4 (99214) and level-5 (99215) established-patient E&M codes when documentation supported only level-2 or level-3 codes. The Corporate Integrity Agreement requires (a) Independent Review Organization-led coding audits annually for 5 years, (b) revision of EHR templates to reduce auto-population that contributed to overdocumentation, (c) compliance officer reports to the OIG quarterly, (d) targeted re-training for the 14 providers identified in the sample. This is the second 7-figure settlement of 2026 referencing EHR-template-driven upcoding.
